title: Capstone: Build a Graph State Orchestrator
concept: Implementing a custom state-machine graph orchestrator that executes a planning-coding-review loop; 'Next up: Agentic Coding.'.

exercises.prompt: What do we measure when auditing the performance of an orchestrator?
exercises.explain: When auditing the performance of an orchestrator, we specifically look at its memory footprint.

cards.title: Building an Orchestrator
cards.body: A Graph State Orchestrator is essential for managing and executing multiple states in a planned sequence, making them work together seamlessly.

cards.title: What is a State-Machine Graph?
cards.body: A state-machine graph is a visual representation of the different states an application can be in and the transitions between those states.

cards.title: Like a Traffic Light
cards.body: Imagine a traffic light at an intersection. Each light (state) changes based on what’s happening (inputs), guiding cars (actions) on when to stop, go, or slow down.

cards.title: Real-World Application
cards.body: In coding reviews, the orchestrator can manage states like 'Code Review', 'Feedback', and 'Merge', moving from one to the next based on the review process.

cards.title: Myth: Complex Orchestrators Are Always Better
cards.body: Not true! Sometimes simpler orchestrators can perform just as well with less resource usage.

cards.title: Visualizing States
cards.body: Graphs help us see how states interact and transition for clearer planning.

right.text: How to move to the next state.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of a Graph State Orchestrator?

options.text: To manage and execute multiple states

exercises.explain: The primary goal of a Graph State Orchestrator is to effectively manage and execute multiple states in a defined sequence.

exercises.prompt: A Graph State Orchestrator can only handle two states at a time.
exercises.explain: False! A Graph State Orchestrator can manage multiple states simultaneously, allowing for more complex behavior and planning.

exercises.prompt: A state-machine graph is a visual representation of different _____ and transitions.
exercises.sentence: A state-machine graph is a visual representation of different states and transitions.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'states' as it depicts various phases of the application and how they link together.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes a Graph State Orchestrator's role?
exercises.explain: The correct description is to manage the workflow between states, facilitating a smooth transition and execution.

exercises.prompt: What is one key feature of a custom state-machine Graph Orchestrator?

options.text: It eliminates the need for planning

exercises.explain: The main feature of a custom Graph Orchestrator is its ability to execute a planning-coding-review loop, allowing systematic progression through connected stages.
